acquisition method: The instrument methods employed for LTQ Orbitrap Velos were set up in DDA mode.
alkylation reagent: alkylated with iodoacetamide (50 mM for 30 min at room temperature, in the dark)
alkylation concentration: alkylated with iodoacetamide (50 mM for 30 min at room temperature, in the dark)
cleavage agent: digested with trypsin (1 µg) for 16 h at 37 °C.
collision energy: fragmented in higher-energy collisional dissociation (HCD) with a normalized collision energy of 40%.
fragmentation method: fragmented in higher-energy collisional dissociation (HCD) with a normalized collision energy of 40%.
instrument: analyzed using an LTQ Orbitrap Velos (Thermo Fisher Scientific) mass spectrometer.
ionization type: with a Proxeon nanoelectrospray ion source.
labeling: The glycosylation sites were confirmed by LC-MS/MS.
reduction reagent: were reduced with dithiothreitol (10 mM for 30 min)
reduction concentration: were reduced with dithiothreitol (10 mM for 30 min)
